properties.name: Atacama Lithium Triangle
properties.geopoliticalNotes: World's largest lithium reserve. China holds equity stakes in SQM via Tianqi Lithium. Critical to EV battery supply chains.

properties.name: Bolivian Lithium Salar
properties.geopoliticalNotes: World's largest known lithium deposit. Nationalized under Morales government. Chinese & Russian firms negotiating access.

properties.name: Puna Lithium (Argentina)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Part of the Lithium Triangle. Chinese firm Ganfeng holds significant stake.

properties.name: Pilbara Lithium (Greenbushes)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: World's highest-grade lithium hard rock mine. Tianqi Lithium (China) holds 51% via joint venture.

properties.name: Qinghai Lithium Brine
properties.geopoliticalNotes: China controls ~60% of global lithium processing. Domestic supply base reduces import dependency.

properties.name: DRC Cobalt Belt (Katanga)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: DRC supplies ~70% of global cobalt. Chinese firms (CMOC, Zhejiang Huayou) control major mines. Artisanal mining raises ESG concerns.

properties.name: Philippine Cobalt (Palawan)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Philippines is a key cobalt source in Southeast Asia. Chinese processing firms are primary off-take buyers.

properties.name: Bayan Obo Rare Earths
properties.geopoliticalNotes: World's largest rare earth deposit. China controls ~85% of global rare earth processing. Export restrictions used as geopolitical leverage.

properties.name: Mount Weld Rare Earths
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Highest grade rare earth deposit outside China. Lynas is critical to US/Allied rare earth supply chain diversification.

properties.name: Mountain Pass Rare Earths
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Only active US rare earth mine. DoD investment to restore domestic supply chain. MP Materials expanding into magnet manufacturing.

properties.name: Escondida Copper Mine
properties.geopoliticalNotes: World's largest copper mine. Chile supplies ~27% of global copper. Periodic labour disputes and water scarcity risks.

properties.name: Las Bambas Copper (Peru)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Chinese state-owned MMG acquired from Glencore. Frequent community blockades disrupt shipments. Key to China's copper security strategy.

properties.name: Konkola / Nchanga Copper (Zambia)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Zambia's Copperbelt supplies ~4% of global copper. Political instability and power shortages risk output. Chinese firms expanding presence.

properties.name: Sulawesi Nickel (Morowali)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Indonesia banned nickel ore exports in 2020 forcing downstream processing. China dominates the IMIP industrial park with ~30 smelters. Key to EV battery supply chain.

properties.name: Norilsk Nickel (Russia)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Russia produces ~7% of global nickel and ~40% of global palladium. Sanctions limit Western access. Supplying China and India as alternative markets.

properties.name: Balama Graphite (Mozambique)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: World's largest graphite deposit. Critical for lithium-ion battery anodes. Insurgency in Cabo Delgado province poses security risk.

properties.name: Mahenge Graphite (Tanzania)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: One of Africa's largest graphite deposits. Under development with backing from Australian and UK investors.

properties.name: Chinese Graphite (Shandong/Heilongjiang)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: China produces ~65% of global graphite and controls ~90% of battery-grade graphite processing. Export restrictions imposed Oct 2023 as tech war escalates.

properties.name: South African Manganese (Kalahari)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: South Africa holds ~80% of global manganese reserves. Critical for steel and EV battery cathodes (LMFP chemistry).

properties.name: Gabon Manganese (Moanda)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: One of the world's highest grade manganese deposits. French firm Eramet owns COMILOG. Rail infrastructure critical to export logistics.

properties.name: Australian Titanium (Murray Basin)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Australia is world's largest producer of ilmenite and rutile. Critical for aerospace, pigments, and future nuclear applications.

properties.name: South African Titanium (Richards Bay)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: One of the world's largest mineral sands operations. Powering global titanium dioxide pigment markets.

properties.name: Stillwater Platinum-Palladium
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Only PGM mine in the US. Critical for catalytic converters and hydrogen fuel cells. South African firm Sibanye owns it.

properties.name: Bushveld PGM Complex
properties.geopoliticalNotes: South Africa holds ~90% of world platinum reserves. Russia (Norilsk) supplies ~40% of palladium. Together they dominate PGM supply critical to automotive and hydrogen sectors.

properties.name: Voisey's Bay Nickel-Cobalt
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Canadian nickel-cobalt anchor for North American battery supply chain. US IRA incentivises processing closer to home. Vale converting to underground.

properties.name: Cobalt Ontario (Canada)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Historic mining district. Renewed interest due to cobalt's role in EV batteries. Allied supply chain diversification from DRC.

properties.name: Hpakant Jade / REE (Myanmar)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Myanmar supplies ~50% of world heavy rare earth elements used in permanent magnets. Operations controlled by Chinese firms amid civil war. Revenue finances military junta.

properties.name: Yunnan REE & Tin
properties.geopoliticalNotes: China's southern REE provinces supply heavy rare earths. Strict government quotas and export controls maintain pricing leverage.

properties.name: US Virgin Islands Bauxite (regional)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Jamaica is a major bauxite exporter. Russian firm Rusal owns Jamalco. Gallium (critical for semiconductors) is a bauxite byproduct.

properties.name: Sarawak Bauxite (Malaysia)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Malaysia was major bauxite exporter to China before 2016 moratorium due to environmental damage. Partial resumption ongoing.

properties.name: Jiangxi Tungsten
properties.geopoliticalNotes: China supplies ~80% of global tungsten. Critical for hardened steel tools, military projectiles, and semiconductor manufacturing. Export controls imposed 2023.

properties.name: Bolivian Tungsten (Chorolque)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Bolivia is world's 3rd largest tungsten producer. Offers Western supply diversification from China.

properties.name: Molycorp / Questa Molybdenum
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Questa mine closed 2014. Mo is critical for high-strength steel alloys used in defense and aerospace. US dependent on imports.

properties.name: Kola Peninsula Apatite (Russia)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Russia is world's 2nd largest phosphate fertilizer exporter. Sanctions disrupted global fertilizer markets in 2022. Also co-produces REEs.

properties.name: Waad Al-Shamal Phosphate (Saudi Arabia)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Saudi Arabia diversifying beyond oil into critical minerals. World's 2nd largest known phosphate reserves. Key to global food security.

properties.name: Pedernales Bauxite (Dominican Republic)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Secondary aluminum source for North American markets.

properties.name: Kovdor Phoscorite (Russia)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Produces iron ore, apatite, and baddeleyite (zirconium). Located 5km from Finnish border.

properties.name: Athabasca Uranium (Saskatchewan)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Canada and Kazakhstan supply ~50% of world uranium. Cameco's Cigar Lake is world's highest grade uranium deposit. Critical for nuclear energy security.

properties.name: Kazakh Uranium (Chu-Sarysu Basin)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Kazakhstan supplies ~42% of global uranium. Kazatomprom is world's largest uranium producer. Russia's Rosatom holds minority stakes. Western utilities seeking supply diversification.

properties.name: Lumwana Copper (Zambia)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Barrick Gold expanding Lumwana capacity to 240Kt/yr. Part of broader Zambia copper belt expansion.

properties.name: Fukuoka Gallium (Japan recycling)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Japan recycles and refines gallium and indium from imported bauxite and zinc. China export controls on gallium (Jul 2023) triggered emergency stockpiling.

properties.name: Chongqing Vanadium
properties.geopoliticalNotes: China produces ~57% of global vanadium, critical for grid-scale vanadium redox flow batteries and high-strength steel alloys used in aerospace and construction.

properties.name: Zimbabwe Lithium (Bikita)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Zimbabwe holds world's 5th largest lithium reserves. Chinese firms (Sinomine, Huayou) have secured major concessions. Western concern over exclusionary deals.

properties.name: Thacker Pass Lithium (Nevada)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Largest known lithium deposit in US. General Motors invested $650M. Critical for North American EV battery supply chain under IRA. First production expected 2026-27.

properties.name: Finnish Cobalt-Nickel (Terrafame)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Finland's Terrafame is sole European nickel-cobalt producer supplying battery grade materials. Critical for EU battery sovereignty under Critical Raw Materials Act.

properties.name: Svalbard Coal & REE
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Norwegian REE deposits in Svalbard and mainland gaining strategic interest. Geopolitically sensitive due to Russia's coal mining rights on Svalbard under 1920 Treaty.

properties.name: Ukrainian Titanium & Lithium
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Ukraine holds Europe's largest lithium reserves and significant titanium deposits. Russian occupation of eastern oblasts covers mineral-rich areas. US-Ukraine critical minerals deal signed 2025.

properties.name: Chiang Rai Rare Earths (Thailand)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Thailand produces tin and monazite (REE source). Historically significant in Southeast Asian mineral supply chains.

properties.name: Korean Tungsten (Sangdong)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Sangdong is one of world's largest tungsten deposits. Strategically important for Korean and allied defense supply chains. Backed by Korean government to reduce China dependency.

properties.name: Saudi Phosphate (Manifa / Al Jalamid)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Ma'aden Vision 2030 plans to grow fertilizer exports, reducing reliance on oil revenues. Strategic food security asset.

properties.name: Colombian Emeralds & Gold
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Colombia supplies ~55% of world emeralds. Illegal gold mining by FARC dissidents and criminal groups is persistent. Conflict mineral risk.

properties.name: Rwandan Coltan / Tantalum
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Tantalum used in capacitors for smartphones, EVs, aerospace. DRC conflict minerals often smuggled via Rwanda. 3TG conflict minerals audit requirements apply.

properties.name: Armenian Copper-Molybdenum (Kajaran)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Armenia's Kajaran deposit near Azerbaijan border. Geopolitical tensions with Azerbaijan risk supply disruption. Rhenium byproduct critical for jet engine alloys.

properties.name: Quebrada Blanca Copper (Chile)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: QB2 expansion operational since 2023. One of largest copper expansions globally in a decade. Water sourced from ocean desalination.

properties.name: Oman Chromite
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Oman's ophiolite belt is one of the world's best exposed chromite provinces. Critical for stainless steel and defense armor plating.

properties.name: Brazilian Niobium (Araxá)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Brazil supplies ~92% of global niobium used in high-strength steel for autos, bridges, pipelines. CBMM (Moreira Salles family) dominates market. China is largest buyer.

properties.name: Scandinavian REE (Norra Kärr, Sweden)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: EU Critical Raw Materials Act priority project. Norra Kärr could supply European magnet manufacturers and reduce Chinese dependency for EU defense and clean energy.

properties.name: Kiruna LKAB Iron & REE (Sweden)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: LKAB announced 2022 Europe's largest REE deposit as a byproduct of iron ore processing. Could supply >30% of EU REE demand. Green steel ambitions via hydrogen-based HYBRIT process.

properties.name: Chilean Copper South (El Teniente)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: World's largest underground copper mine. State-owned Codelco faces aging infrastructure challenges. Water scarcity in Atacama region is growing operational risk.

properties.name: Laos Potash (Khemmarat Basin)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Massive unexplored potash reserves. Critical for global fertilizer security. Chinese firms active in exploration. Under the shadow of Mekong dam geopolitics.

properties.name: Tanzania REE (Songwe Hill)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Tanzania's Songwe Hill is one of East Africa's most advanced REE projects. Western-backed to diversify from Chinese supply.

properties.name: Namibian Uranium (Rössing / Husab)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Namibia is world's 4th largest uranium producer. Husab mine (Chinese CGNPC) is world's 3rd largest. Western utilities increasingly looking to Namibia post-Russia sanctions.

properties.name: US Rare Earth Montana (Bear Lodge)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: US government backed rare earth deposit. Bear Lodge would be 2nd US REE mine after Mountain Pass. DoD funding exploration to reduce import reliance.

properties.name: Kyrgyz Gold-REE (Kumtor / Kutessay)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Kumtor is Central Asia's largest gold mine. Kutessay II is Central Asia's only REE mine, now being revived. Russian sphere of influence through CSTO membership.

properties.name: Brazilian Lithium (Araçuaí / Pegmatites)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Brazil's Green Lithium producer Sigma Lithium targets North American IRA-eligible supply chain. Brazil joining BRICS complicates geopolitical alignment.

properties.name: Mariana Trench Polymetallic Nodules
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Deep sea mining remains contested. Nodules on Pacific Ocean floor contain billions of tonnes of critical minerals. ISA moratorium debate ongoing. China, Russia, and US companies hold exploration licenses.

properties.name: Brazilian Iron Ore (Carajás)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: World's largest iron ore complex. Vale is world's largest iron ore producer. Brazil-China trade axis dominant in iron ore pricing. Tailings dam failures (Mariana 2015, Brumadinho 2019) raised ESG concerns.

properties.name: Pilbara Iron Ore (Hamersley)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Australia supplies ~56% of global iron ore exports, overwhelmingly to China. Green iron / hydrogen DRI technology being piloted at Pilbara. Strategic decarbonisation asset.

properties.name: Pakistani Copper (Reko Diq)
properties.geopoliticalNotes: One of world's largest undeveloped copper deposits. Long-delayed due to legal disputes. Barrick Gold now leading development. Security risks in Balochistan insurgency zone.

properties.name: Baja California Lithium-Cesium
properties.geopoliticalNotes: Mexico nationalized lithium in 2022 (Litio Para México). Geothermal lithium extraction from Salton Sea-type brines. North American supply chain candidate under USMCA.
